item_name: Mascot Creation Workflow with AI (consistency, character bible)
definition_origin: An AI-augmented mascot workflow is a repeatable pipeline that turns 'I want a character' into a consistent, multi-pose, multi-expression, multi-context character library suitable for app states, onboarding, marketing, push-notification illustrations, and merchandise. Emerged 2023-2024 as Stable Diffusion + IP-Adapter + LoRA + Midjourney --cref/--sref made character consistency tractable for solo devs without illustrators.
core_purpose: Mascots solve the brand-personality problem — they make abstract apps feel like a relationship rather than a tool. Visceral: instant warmth/joy/curiosity from a face users meet on first launch. Behavioral: the mascot becomes feedback (Duo cheers on streak, frowns on miss) — creating an emotional accountability loop. Reflective: users tell stories about 'my Duo' — the mascot becomes the brand mnemonic. The character bible (locked traits + reference sheet + pose library) is what prevents drift across 6 months of generated assets.
when_needed: Consumer mobile (especially habit-formation, learning, kids, wellness, pet, finance-for-fun): nearly mandatory for retention — Duolingo, Headspace's character-less calm, Calm's nature mascot-substitute, Finch, Habitica all anchor on mascot-or-character-system. AI-wrapper apps: increasingly common — Pi.ai, Character.ai, Replika are character-first. B2B/dev-tool: rare and risky — Slack Slackbot, GitHub Octocat work because they're spare; over-mascotted dev tools read as childish. Marketing: yes for consumer, no for enterprise sales.
format_options: Static 2D vector (SVG) for in-app illustrations — small, theme-able. Static 2D raster (PNG/WebP) for marketing where vector isn't needed. Lottie (.json/.lottie) for animated mascot states (see Item 19). Rive (.riv) for state-machine interactive mascot. 3D model (glTF/USDZ) for hero brand moments. Sprite sheets (PNG atlas) for frame-by-frame fallback. Reference images (PNG) for the character bible itself — locked at 1024×1024 with consistent backgrounds. Storage convention: /brand/mascot/bible/ (locked refs), /brand/mascot/poses/ (production assets), /brand/mascot/animated/ (Lottie/Rive). Source-of-truth recommendation: keep a Figma/Notion 'Character Bible' page as canonical, with file-paths to each asset.
file_size_performance: Reference images: 1024x1024 PNG, ~500KB-1.5MB each (kept out of app bundle, in /brand/ assets). Production SVG: 5-30 KB per pose. PNG raster mascot: 50-200 KB at 2x retina. Lottie mascot: 10-50 KB per state. Pose library: 9-16 expressions + 10-20 poses = ~25-40 reference images, 30-50 production assets. Total brand-mascot bundle for app: target ≤500 KB across all in-app uses.
free_tools: Imagen 4 / Whisk (free with Gemini Pro): high-quality character generation, supports reference-image conditioning via Whisk, strong at illustration style. Midjourney (paid only — $10-30/mo, see paid tier). Stable Diffusion via ComfyUI (free, local, requires GPU): the gold standard for character consistency via IP-Adapter FaceID + LoRA. Leonardo.ai (free tier: 150 credits/day, character reference feature). Recraft (free tier: 50 credits/day, vector-style outputs, best-in-class style reference). Krea.ai (free tier with watermark, real-time generation). Civitai (free model + LoRA library). RunDiffusion (free tier limited credits, hosted ComfyUI without local GPU). Tensor.art (free tier with daily credits). Flux.1 Schnell (free local, Apache 2.0). HuggingFace Spaces (free public ComfyUI/Flux demos). For training a character LoRA without local GPU: Replicate ($0.0014/sec for training, ~$2-5 for a full LoRA), or HuggingFace AutoTrain. For vector conversion: Vectorizer.AI (free 5/mo), Recraft vector mode, Adobe Express free tier.
paid_tools: Midjourney Basic ($10/mo): --cref + --sref + --ow (omni-reference for v7), industry-standard character consistency. Midjourney Standard ($30/mo): unlimited fast hours, worth it during character-bible creation phase, downgrade after. Recraft Pro ($12/mo): vector outputs + style references + brand-kit, best for app illustrations directly. Replicate (~$5-15 to train a custom character LoRA, $0.001/run inference). Tiering: starter $10-15/mo (Midjourney Basic OR Recraft Pro) — sufficient. Pro $30/mo (Midjourney Standard during bible-creation, then downgrade). Enterprise $50+/mo (Midjourney Pro + Recraft Pro + Replicate budget) — only for ongoing character-system work.
gemini_pro_workflow: Step 1 — Define personality with Gemini Pro chat: prompt 'You are a brand strategist. My app is {product}. Define a mascot with: 5 personality traits, Jungian archetype, body proportions (cute-fat / lanky / chibi / realistic), 3-color palette in hex, distinctive silhouette feature, and an enemy/foil character.' Save the output verbatim — this is your character bible header. Step 2 — Imagen 4 base reference: prompt 'Character design sheet of {character_name}, a {species} with {distinctive_features}, {palette_hex}, flat 2D vector illustration style, single-weight outline, behance trending mascot design, isolated on white background, centered, full body, neutral T-pose facing camera, 1024x1024'. Generate 12 variants, pick canonical. Step 3 — Use Whisk with chosen reference + new prompts to build the expression grid: 'same character, three-quarter view, smiling joyfully' / 'side profile, sad with single tear' / 'back view walking away'. Step 4 — Gemini Pro multimodal critique: paste 6 generated poses + ask 'Identify inconsistencies: facial features, palette drift, proportion changes, line weight differences. List specific corrections.' Step 5 — Lock prompt template + reference images in a Gem ('CharacterBibleGuard' Gem with system prompt 'You are the keeper of {character} canon. Given a new pose request, output the exact Imagen prompt with character description prefix locked, only varying action, expression, and context'). Step 6 — Veo for animation reference if you'll be doing Lottie character motion later.
workflow_steps: PHASE 1 — Character Bible (Day 1, 4-6 hr). 1. Personality definition (30 min): 5 traits + archetype + body proportions + palette + foil. Write it in plaintext you'll paste into every prompt. 2. Base reference generation (60 min): 12-16 Imagen variants, pick 1 canonical front-view. 3. Character sheet (90 min): generate front / 3/4 / side / back views maintaining canon — use Imagen + Whisk reference, OR Midjourney --cref --cw 100. 4. Expression grid (90 min): 9-16 expressions in 4x4 grid (joy, sad, angry, confused, focused, sleeping, surprised, embarrassed, neutral, laughing, crying, scared, proud, tired, sick, excited). 5. Pose library (90 min): 10-20 poses (idle, walking, jumping, sitting, waving, pointing, thinking, celebrating, hiding, sleeping, eating, falling, dancing, reading, looking-up, peeking). 6. Lock the bible: export all references to /brand/mascot/bible/, write the 'character canon' doc with description + 5 traits + palette + 'never do' list. PHASE 2 — Production Asset Generation (per-asset, 15-45 min). 7. Select pose+expression closest to need from bible. 8. Generate variants conditioned on bible reference (Imagen+Whisk / Midjourney --cref / ComfyUI IP-Adapter FaceID + LoRA). 9. Critique pass via Gemini multimodal — flag drift. 10. Vectorize chosen variant (Recraft / Vectorizer.AI / hand-trace in Figma) — never ship raw AI raster. 11. Color-token replace via SVG class replacement for theming. PHASE 3 — Consistency Maintenance. 12. Quarterly bible audit: regenerate bible refs against any tool/model upgrade. 13. Train a character LoRA on Replicate ($5) once you have 25+ approved bible images for ironclad consistency.
ai_prompt_templates: Bible-base prompt: 'Character design sheet of {character_name}, a {species_or_type} with {distinguishing_silhouette_feature}, color palette {hex1} {hex2} {hex3}, body proportions {proportion_ratio_e_g_3-head-tall}, personality {trait1} {trait2} {trait3}, flat 2D vector illustration, single-weight outline 4px, no gradients, no glassmorphism, isolated on transparent background, behance trending mascot design, 1024x1024.' Pose-variation prompt (Midjourney): '{character_canonical_description}, {action}, {expression}, {camera_angle}, isolated on white --cref {bible_url} --cw 100 --sref {style_url} --ar 1:1 --v 6.0'. Pose-variation prompt (Imagen+Whisk): paste bible reference image + 'same character as reference, now {action} with {expression}, {camera_angle}, identical proportions and palette and outline weight'. Expression-grid prompt: 'Expression sheet, 4x4 grid of 16 facial expressions of {character}: joy, sadness, anger, surprise, fear, disgust, contempt, neutral, focused, confused, embarrassed, proud, sleepy, sick, mischievous, loving — same head pose three-quarter view, identical proportions, vector style, on white grid background.' Critique prompt (Gemini): 'Compare these {N} generated images of {character}. Score each 0-100 on: facial-feature consistency, palette adherence, proportion match, line-weight match, overall canon-fit. Flag the worst offender with 3 specific fixes.' LoRA-training caption template: 'a flat 2D mascot illustration of {character_name}, a {species}, {feature}, {palette}, single-weight outline, white background, behance editorial style, {pose}, {expression}'.
claude_code_integration: Repo structure: /brand/mascot/bible/ (refs locked, gitignored if large), /apps/*/src/assets/mascot/{state}.{svg,lottie,riv}/. Claude Code can: (1) generate <Mascot state="happy" size={120} /> typed wrapper component that picks the right asset by state-name; (2) write a CI check that verifies mascot palette tokens exist in the design-tokens file; (3) generate Storybook stories showing every mascot state in light+dark modes; (4) write a script that runs Gemini multimodal critique on every newly added mascot SVG and flags drift in PR review; (5) generate prefers-reduced-motion fallback (animated → static) automatically; (6) emit accessibility-label maps ('happy' → 'mascot smiling joyfully'); (7) auto-optimize SVGs with svgo and Lotties with @lottiefiles/lottie-optimizer.
emotional_design_integration: Caregiver (Headspace's color-blob, Calm's nature, Finch's pet, Reflectly): soft round body, large eyes, slow blinking, breathing animation, gentle palette. Magician (Replika, Pi.ai, Genie): ethereal/abstract form, particle aura, transformative motion, gradient interior. Jester (Duolingo's Duo, Discord's Wumpus, Slack's Slackbot historically): exaggerated proportions, expressive face, rubber-band motion, bright primaries. Hero (Strava, Habitica): bold confident silhouette, action poses, triumphant expressions. Innocent (Zenly's flower, kid-app mascots): childlike round forms, simple geometry, primary palette. Outlaw (rare in apps, more in gaming): asymmetric, edgy, broken silhouettes, dark palette. Sage (rare; Notion's notion-face, Stripe's typographic mark): minimal, restrained, often non-character. Lover (dating, beauty): soft warm palette, sensual curves. Test: in 1 second, can a stranger guess the archetype from silhouette alone? If not, refine.
specific_examples: Duolingo's Duo (https://www.duolingo.com) — peak Jester mascot system: 100+ states, animated Lotties, push-notification character, evolved into recognizable IP. Body: green owl, distinctive silhouette, expressive face. Headspace's color-blobs (https://www.headspace.com) — characterless Caregiver: shapes-as-mascots, breathing motion. Finch (https://finchcare.com) — Caregiver pet bird, tightly consistent pose library, app-state-driven mascot. Habitica (https://habitica.com) — pixel-art RPG character system. Replika (https://replika.com) — 3D Magician avatar, customizable. Pi.ai (https://pi.ai) — ethereal abstract Magician orb. Slackbot (https://slack.com) — minimalist Sage mascot, restrained. GitHub Octocat (https://octodex.github.com) — exemplary character bible: 100+ canonical Octocat variations all on-brand. Mailchimp's Freddie — restrained Jester. Discord's Wumpus — Jester. Reflectly's character — Caregiver. Counter-examples: every 'generic chibi fox' indie app — undifferentiated.
anti_ai_slop_traps: (1) Generic chibi-style fox/cat/owl with oversized head (3-head-tall body) — looks identical across every Civitai cute-mascot LoRA. Counter: choose a less-overused species (axolotl, capybara, pangolin, blob-fish, slime-mold) OR exaggerate one feature radically (one big ear, asymmetric eye, missing tooth). (2) AI-default 'kawaii' face (round eyes + dot mouth) — homogeneous slop. Counter: design a distinctive eye shape (square, comma, asterisk) and lock it in bible. (3) Six-fingered hands / melted appendages — hide hands in mittens, gloves, behind back, OR redraw vector by hand. (4) Background gradient mesh / glassmorphic blob behind the character — instant 2024 AI tell. Counter: solid color or transparent only. (5) Mismatched proportions across poses (head shrinks/grows) — use --cref --cw 100 OR train a LoRA. (6) Palette drift (purple becomes pink-purple becomes magenta) — always specify exact hex codes in prompts AND post-process with color-replace. (7) 'AI girl with anime eyes' default for human characters — escape it by going non-human or stylizing to abstract proportions. (8) Default Stable Diffusion 'plastic skin' — use Flux or stylize to flat-vector to dodge it. Counter-prompts to append: 'NO generic chibi proportions, NO kawaii dot-mouth, NO gradient mesh background, NO glassmorphism, NO six fingers, distinctive {asymmetric_feature}, exact palette {hex1} {hex2} {hex3}, single-weight outline, flat vector illustration, behance editorial mascot design.'
common_pitfalls: Bible-skipping: solo devs generate poses ad-hoc without a locked bible — produces 50 variants of slightly different characters. Always do bible first. Vector-skipping: shipping raw AI raster — JPEG artifacts, melted edges, can't theme — always vectorize. Over-mascotting: putting the mascot on every screen kills its specialness — reserve for emotional moments (success, empty state, onboarding finale, error recovery). Inconsistent voice: mascot face drifts as different people generate — designate one bible-keeper. State-mismatch: mascot looks happy on an error screen — build a state→expression map. Accessibility neglect: animated mascot violates reduced-motion — always provide static fallback. Cultural insensitivity: certain colors/animals/gestures read negatively in markets — review with native speakers before launch. Legal: AI-generated character may be unregistrable as trademark in some jurisdictions (US Copyright Office stance evolving) — for an actual IP asset, redraw by human hand to claim authorship. Scope creep: 80 hours on bible creation when 16 is sufficient.
accessibility_notes: Every mascot instance needs descriptive alt text by state ('mascot celebrating' not 'mascot'). Honor prefers-reduced-motion: serve static expression instead of animated. Color-not-only-signal: don't rely on mascot's expression alone for state — pair with text/icon (mascot frowning + 'Network error' text). Screen-reader: decorative mascots aria-hidden=true; semantic mascots (e.g. mascot communicates achievement) aria-label. Voice description: build a copy-deck mapping each mascot state to a text description.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: ship SVG or Lottie at 2x/3x retina sizes; bundle smaller pose library (8-12 critical states); use react-native-svg or react-native-fast-image. Web: can lazy-load larger pose library; provide higher-density assets via srcset; CDN-cache. AR/3D mascot: USDZ on iOS Quick Look enables 'mascot in your room' marketing moments. Push notifications: iOS supports rich notifications with image — render the mascot at the right state into the push image. Marketing: full character sheets and animated reveals OK on web; in-app, restraint always wins.
sources: [ComfyUI Character Consistency Guide 2026 (Apatero)](https://www.apatero.com/blog/comfyui-character-consistency-advanced-workflows-2026); [ComfyUI LoRA Training for Characters 2026 (Apatero)](https://www.apatero.com/blog/comfyui-lora-training-character-consistency-guide-2026); [Stable Diffusion Character Consistency 2026 (Thinkpeak)](https://thinkpeak.ai/stable-diffusion-character-consistency-tutorial/); [How I Solved Character Consistency in ComfyUI (Sophie Z, Medium)](https://medium.com/@sophie_62065/how-i-solved-character-consistency-in-comfyui-after-trying-controlnet-and-ipadapter-fcd9eda25109); [ComfyUI IPAdapter Plus GitHub](https://github.com/cubiq/ComfyUI_IPAdapter_plus); [Midjourney Character Reference docs](https://docs.midjourney.com/hc/en-us/articles/32162917505293-Character-Reference); [Mastering --cref (Bootcamp Medium)](https://medium.com/design-bootcamp/mastering-midjourney-character-reference-cref-678f0990ae68); [Character Consistency Brand Mascots (Aimensa)](https://aimensa.com/character-consistency-midjourney-fixed-parameters); [GitHub Octodex (canonical character bible)](https://octodex.github.com)
